definition:
1. to plan  or contrive against , κακὸν πόλει (Tyrtaeus Elegiacus); θάνατόν τινι (Herdotus Historicus):—;with dative pers. only, to plot against, lay snares for , τῇ πόλει (Aeschulus Tragicus); τῷ πλήθει (Aristophanes Comicus);—;absolute, οὑπιβουλεύων the conspirer , (Sophocles Tragicus): —;with accusative rei only, to plan secretly, scheme, plot , τὸν ἔκπλουν (Thucydides) 
2. with dative rei, to form designs upon, aim at , πρήγμασι μεγάλοισι (Herdotus Historicus); τυραννίδι (Plato Philosophus) 
3. with infinitive to purpose  or design to  do, (Herdotus Historicus) 
4. Pass., with future middle -εύσομαι: aor1 -εβουλεύθην:—; to have plots formed against one, to be the object of plots , (Thucydides) 
5. of things, to be designed against , πρᾶγμα, ὃ τοῖς θεοῖς ἐπιβουλεύεται (Aristophanes Comicus); τὰ ἐπιβουλευόμενα plots , (Xenophon Historicus) (ML)
